text: Code reviews are a vital part of software development. During a code review, a developer checks another team member’s code before it is added to the main project. This simple process helps catch bugs early, before they become bigger problems. However, the benefits go beyond finding mistakes. Code reviews also encourage knowledge sharing because team members learn from each other’s techniques and solutions. Over time, this practice improves overall team quality. Everyone becomes more familiar with the project, and consistent standards emerge. In short, regular code reviews create stronger, more reliable code and a more collaborative team.
